mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts show a positive return frequency below 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP and retail sales indicates a broad-based demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in GDP and retail sales fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive return frequency exceeds 60% during co-contractions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5% reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following industrial production surges show a positive return frequency below 52%, or the signal reverses direction during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.6% signals accelerating price pressures that erode real corporate margins and trigger defensive portfolio rotation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months yield positive 60-day returns in over 58% of cases, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate income and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a frequency above 53%, or the signal shows no statistical edge with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized strength in durable goods demand and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-expansions in housing and retail sales produce negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the joint signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly declines in industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a frequency above 55%, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 in rolling validation.
